title: interview intent
description: Structured campaign intent produced by an interview between the hpc agent and either MARs or a human. Consumed by `hpc-mapreduce interview --intent <file>` to materialize tasks.py + meta.json + interview.json. The interview.json artifact is the persistence of this same payload (plus a derived cmd_sha) so future `cmd_recall` queries can replay it.

goal.description: Free-text campaign goal, ~one sentence. Captures intent that the structured fields can't (e.g. 'find LR for vit-b on imagenet-1k @ 8 GPUs').

search.description: Structured search space; the materializer turns this into a tasks.py.

budget.description: Soft caps. campaign-flow surfaces these in its progress envelope; it does not enforce them by killing jobs.

cluster_target.description: Optional: when present, submit_flow uses these directly. When omitted, the planner is invoked to choose.

abort_if.description: Optional early-stop criterion. campaign-flow consumes this; interview just records it. Exactly one of `above` / `below` should be supplied.

produced_by.description: Who/what produced this intent. Indexed by `cmd_recall` so future interviews can surface 'last time you ran this kind of campaign…' summaries.

notes.description: Free-form supplementary notes from the interview transcript (caveats, deferred decisions, things the human said 'maybe' about).

axes.description: Each key is a parameter name; each value is the list of values to sweep. tasks = full cross product.

tasks_py.description: Path to a hand-rolled tasks.py the agent has produced separately. The materializer copies it verbatim and skips the validation cross-check on (low, high, n) since manual shapes don't expose those parameters.

session_sha.description: MARs session identifier. Required when kind=mars.

operator.description: Human operator name/handle when kind=human; null for MARs.
